key_points: Bholi’s silence symbolises oppression caused by social neglect and patriarchy
Her identity shifts from ‘Bholi’ to ‘Sulekha’ through education
The school represents hope, reform, and liberation
The teacher challenges traditional attitudes through compassion
The wedding scene acts as the moral climax of the story
Bholi’s refusal is an assertion of self-worth and autonomy
characters: Bholi (Sulekha) – a symbol of empowered womanhood
Ramlal – a victim of social conformity
The Teacher – an agent of social reform
Bishamber Nath – embodiment of patriarchal greed
important_events: Public refusal redefining success for women
Decision to serve parents and teach
message: True empowerment lies in education and self-respect
Social traditions must not suppress human dignity
Women can redefine their destiny through courage
exam_tips: Use symbolism: silence vs speech, school vs home
Analyse the wedding scene as a turning point
Write interpretative answers, not just narration
